splits.title: The Lost City of Nevada
splits.author: Harrison R. Merrill, Brigham Young University
splits.start_boundary: The Lost City of Nevada By Harrison R.
splits.end_boundary: THE RELIEF SOCIETY OF THE CHURCH OF JESUS CHRIST OF LATTER-DAY SAINTS

files.notes: The file contains two distinct pieces: a poem 'At Cedar Breaks' by Kate Thomas and an article 'The Lost City of Nevada' by Harrison R. Merrill. The poem is a spiritual reflection on the natural beauty of Cedar Breaks, while the article describes a visit to archaeological ruins in Nevada and includes historical context about their discovery.

files.notes: The file contains two distinct articles. The first is a long fictional story 'The Master Key' by Minnie Iverson Hodapp about a family's conversion to Mormonism. The second is a practical advice column 'Of Interest to Women' by Lalene H. Hart about making jellies and jams. The boundary between them is clear where the first story ends with 'The End.' and the second begins with the new title and author.

splits.title: Notes from the Field
splits.author: Amy Brown Lyman
splits.start_boundary: Notes from the Field Amy Brown Lyman

splits.title: Extract from the Life of Faith
splits.start_boundary: Extract from the Life of Faith

files.notes: The file contains two distinct articles. The first is a lengthy 'Notes from the Field' report by Amy Brown Lyman covering various Relief Society activities across different stakes and missions. The second is a short poem titled 'Extract from the Life of Faith' with an editor's note. The boundary between them is clear where the first article ends with discussion of the Cottonwood Stake and the second begins with the poem title.

splits.start_boundary: Guide Lessons for November LESSON I Theology and Testimony
splits.end_boundary: other symptoms of nervous or emotional strain?

files.notes: This file contains two distinct sections: a short inspirational poem/article titled 'Extract from the Life of Faith' followed by the complete 'Guide Lessons for November' which contains 4 separate lessons (Theology, Work and Business, Literature on Jean Webster, and Social Service). The Guide Lessons section is a comprehensive educational unit with multiple sub-lessons but should be kept together as one cohesive instructional piece.
